name: Cormac mac Airt
description: Cormac mac Airt, a legendary High King of Ireland, reigns supreme in Irish mythology as a figure of wisdom, justice, and prosperity. His reign, often depicted as a golden age, is characterized by fairness and enlightened rule. Born to Art mac Cuinn, Cormac's early life is shrouded in tales of prophecy and destiny. He is said to have possessed exceptional wisdom from a young age, often dispensing wise judgments and exhibiting keen foresight. Cormac's reign is marked by significant events, including the creation of the Teaghlach Mhór, a grand hall of justice, and the acquisition of magical objects like the Cup of Truth, which would shatter if a lie were spoken in its presence, and the branch of the Silver Apple Tree, whose music could soothe the wounded and the dying. He is associated with the Fianna, the legendary warrior band, and his court was a center of learning and culture. Later in his reign, Cormac faced challenges, including battles and political intrigues, ultimately leading to his demise. Some accounts say he choked on a salmon bone, while others attribute his death to magical circumstances. Despite his tragic end, Cormac mac Airt remains a pivotal figure in Irish lore, embodying the ideal of a wise and just ruler whose legacy continues to inspire.
subtitle: The Wise and Just High King

keyMyths.title: The Cup of Truth
keyMyths.description: Cormac possessed a magical cup that would shatter if a lie was spoken in its presence. This ensured truth and justice in his court. It was said to be a gift from Manannán mac Lir.

keyMyths.title: The Branch of the Silver Apple Tree
keyMyths.description: Cormac also possessed a branch of the Silver Apple Tree that produced soothing music. This music could heal the wounded and comfort the dying. It symbolized his benevolent rule.

keyMyths.title: The Golden Age
keyMyths.description: Cormac's reign is remembered as a golden age of Ireland, marked by peace and prosperity. His wisdom and justice were said to have brought abundance to the land. This era is often romanticized in Irish tales.

sources.author: Geoffrey Keating
sources.work: Foras Feasa ar Éirinn (The History of Ireland)
sources.description: A comprehensive history of Ireland, including accounts of Cormac mac Airt's reign.
sources.text: A comprehensive history of Ireland, including accounts of Cormac mac Airt's reign.

sources.author: Annals of the Four Masters
sources.description: A chronicle of Irish history, containing entries about Cormac's life and reign.
sources.text: A chronicle of Irish history, containing entries about Cormac's life and reign.
